name: Taco Lentils
recipeInstructions:
1. Add lentils to water. Bring mixture to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes or until tender but whole.
2. While the lentils are boiling, remove seeds from the ancho peppers and cover with boiling water. When the peppers are soft, place them in a blender or small food processor with some of the soaking water. Blend until smooth.
3. Add the remaining ingredients to the blended mixture and stir until everything is well combined.
4. Transfer the lentil mixture to a deep baking dish. Pour the tomato sauce over the lentils and stir to combine.
5. Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.
6. Serve in flour or corn tortillas, garnished with desired toppings such as steamed red and green bell pepper slices, onions, jalapeno pepper slices, nonfat cheese, etc.
Note: For the lentil mixture only, per 1/8 recipe: 228 calories, 1.0 g fat, 3.6% CFF. Based on a terrific BBQ Lentil recipe from the USA Dry Pea and Lentil Council and introduced to us by Reggie Dwork.
